paper.title_en: Quantum droplets in dipolar quasi-one-dimensional Bose-Einstein condensates in optical lattices
paper.abstract_en: We consider quantum droplets in dipolar Bose-Einstein condensates embedded in optical lattices within the framework of Gross-Pitaevskii equations. In dipolar BECs, the long-range and isotropic dipole-dipole interaction provides an additional mechanism for self-binding. We analyze the linear stability as well dynamics of quantum droplets. We find an effective potential for the width and show that the optimum width for formation of quantum droplet increases as the dipole-dipole interaction increases. We study dynamics of the stable droplets and see that its width oscillates with time, and the amplitude of oscillation increases with the increase of dipole-dipole interaction. In presence of optical lattices, width of a stable droplet changes quasi-periodically while the location of density profile changes periodically in space. The frequency of oscillation depends sensitively on the lattice parameters. We check dynamical stability of a quantum droplet through numerical simulation.
